zh.Radcliffe, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾拉德克利夫
zh.Coupland, Northumberland, England: 英國諾圖波藍德庫普藍德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Curwen於1190年出生在英國波藍德時，她的父親Sir Alan de Curwen15歲，母親Edith Toogood13歲。她於1210年與Adam de Radcliffe在英國英格蘭藍卡爾拉德克利夫結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1260年在英國諾圖波藍德庫普藍德享年70歲。

zh-hans.Radcliffe, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾拉德克利夫
zh-hans.Coupland, Northumberland, England: 英国諾圖波藍德庫普藍德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Curwen于1190年出生在英国波藍德时，她的父亲Sir Alan de Curwen15岁，母亲Edith Toogood13岁。她于1210年与Adam de Radcliffe在英国英格兰藍卡爾拉德克利夫结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1260年在英国諾圖波藍德庫普藍德享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Curwen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (Lancashire): habitational name from a place called Culewen (now Colvend) in Kirkcudbrightshire. The family was early established in Lancashire, where it was often pronounced without the -w-; see Corrin and Currin .

variants.name: Corrin
meanings: Irish and Manx: shortened form of Mac Torin, from Gaelic mac ‘son (of)’ + Old Norse Thórfinnr (from Thórr, the name of the Scandinavian thunder god, + the ethnic designation Finnr). Compare Turpin . This name has occasionally been confused with Corin .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Curwen was born in 1190, in Cumberland, England, her father, Sir Alan de Curwen, was 15 and her mother, Edith Toogood, was 13. She married Adam de Radcliffe in 1210, in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1260, in Coupland, Northumberland, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Curwen est née en 1190, à Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Alan de Curwen, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Edith Toogood, avait 13 ans. Elle a épousé Adam de Radcliffe en 1210, à Radcliffe, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1260, à Coupland, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Curwen 1190 in Cumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Alan de Curwen 15 und ihre Mutter Edith Toogood 13. Sie hat 1210 in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Adam de Radcliffe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1260 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Coupland, Northumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Curwen è nata il nel 1190 nel Cumberland, England, sua padre, Sir Alan de Curwen, aveva 15 anni e sua madre, Edith Toogood, aveva 13 anni. Lei sposato Adam de Radcliffe nel 1210, nel Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1260, nel Coupland, Northumberland, England, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Curwenが1190年にイギリスクウンバーランドで生まれた時，父Sir Alan de Curwenは15歳，母Edith Toogoodは13歳であった。1210年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアラドクリフでAdam de Radcliffeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1260年にイギリスノースウンバーランドコウプランドで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Curwen(이)가 1190년 영국 c벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Alan de Curwen(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Edith Toogood(은)는 13세였다. 그녀는 1210년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 라드c리프에서 Adam de Radcliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1260년 영국 노르스벌랜드 코우플랜드에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Curwen nasceu em 1190, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Alan de Curwen, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Edith Toogood, tinha 13 anos. Ela casou-se com Adam de Radcliffe em 1210, em Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1260, em Coupland, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Curwen родилась в 1190 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Alan de Curwen было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Edith Toogood, 13. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam de Radcliffe в 1210 г. в местности радклифф, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1260 г. в местности коупланд, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Curwen nació en 1190, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Alan de Curwen, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Edith Toogood, tenía 13 años. Se casó con Adam de Radcliffe en 1210, en Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1260, en Coupland, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años.
